display1.$description: The high-level display text to deliver maximum visual impact as a graphic element in brand or marketing contexts.

when: hero or landing section on desktop where text functions as a visual graphic rather than readable prose
brand or campaign statement limited to a few words requiring the strongest typographic presence
avoid: mobile viewports → display2 or display3
text carrying meaningful readable content → display2 or display3

display2.$description: The mid-level display text to deliver strong marketing messages at the largest readable display scale.

when: primary hero headline requiring the strongest readable typographic presence on desktop
marketing message intended to appear once per page at maximum display emphasis
avoid: mobile viewports → display4 or heading1
cases where display3 scale is sufficient → display3

display3.$description: The mid-level display text to headline landing or hero sections as the default display scale on desktop.

when: primary headline of a landing page or hero section on desktop
marketing statement requiring large visual weight with readable content, intended once per page
avoid: mobile viewports → display4 or heading1
cases requiring stronger impact → display2

display4.$description: The low-level display text to title subsections within a hero or marketing layout at the lowest display hierarchy.

when: subsection headline within a hero or landing area sitting below a display2 or display3 title
largest title in a promotional block that does not require full display1–3 scale
avoid: mobile viewports → heading1 or heading2

heading1.$description: The high-level heading to title landing page sections with the strongest heading scale.

when: section title of a landing page requiring strong typographic emphasis
primary hero headline on mobile where display tokens are not applicable
avoid: general product UI → heading2 or heading3
mobile viewports where heading1 is too large → heading2 or heading3

heading2.$description: The high-level heading to deliver specific messages in product UI or serve as a mobile hero headline.

when: high-emphasis message within a product UI context such as a welcome or onboarding headline
mobile hero headline where heading1 provides more impact than needed
avoid: general informational page titles → heading3
desktop hero headlines → display2, display3, or display4

heading3.$description: The mid-level heading to title general product pages or serve as a secondary title beneath heading1 or heading2.

when: primary title of a general product page in standard UI contexts
secondary title beneath heading1 or heading2 when a lower hierarchy is needed
avoid: marketing or high-emphasis messages → heading2

heading4.$description: The mid-level heading to title sections or serve as the largest title within a contained component.

when: section title within a page layout
primary title inside a dialog, card, or side navigation at the largest component heading scale
avoid: full page main title → heading3

heading5.$description: The low-level heading to title compact components as the default component heading scale.

when: primary title inside a dialog, card, or side navigation at the default component heading scale
short emphatic label requiring stronger visual weight than subtitle or body tokens
avoid: full page main title → heading3
cases requiring larger component heading scale → heading4

heading6.$description: The low-level heading to label the smallest component titles or extra-large component labels.

when: smallest title inside a dialog, card, or side navigation
label for an extra-large component where medium weight is sufficient
avoid: cases requiring bold emphasis → heading5
cases requiring larger component heading scale → heading5

subtitle1.$description: The high-level subtitle to label medium and large components or provide emphasis within body text.

when: label for medium or large components as the default label scale
short text requiring emphasis within a body text context without increasing font size
avoid: body or paragraph text → body2

subtitle2.$description: The low-level subtitle to label small components or serve as a supporting label beneath heading6 or subtitle1.

when: label for small components as the smallest label scale
supporting label paired beneath heading6 or subtitle1
avoid: standalone use without accompanying text or component → subtitle1
body or paragraph text → body2 or body3

body1.$description: The high-level body text to provide supplementary description alongside heading3 or heading4.

when: descriptive text paired directly with heading3 or heading4 as a supporting explanation
placeholder or item label in a large component requiring descriptive text
avoid: general body or paragraph text → body2
supporting text for heading5 or heading6 → body2 or body3

body2.$description: The high-level body text to deliver readable content as the default body text scale.

when: general body or paragraph text in most UI contexts
caption text when used alongside subtitle1
placeholder or item label in a medium component requiring descriptive text
avoid: labels requiring emphasis → subtitle1
supporting description for heading3 or heading4 → body1

body3.$description: The low-level body text to provide descriptive captions for content elements at the default caption scale.

when: caption text describing a title, body, or image element
placeholder or item label in a small component requiring descriptive text
avoid: standalone use without accompanying text or component → body2
general body or paragraph text → body2

body4.$description: The low-level body text to present the smallest caption scale in space-constrained contexts.

when: caption in a space-constrained UI element where no larger size fits
avoid: captions users must read without difficulty → body3
general caption or supplementary description → body3

code1.$description: The high-level code text to display code blocks or inline code at the default code scale.

when: code block or inline code requiring the default code text size
avoid: non-code text → body2 or body3
replacing with sans-serif style is not permitted → always use fontFamily.code

code2.$description: The low-level code text to display code blocks or inline code at the smallest code scale.

when: code block or inline code in a space-constrained context requiring a smaller code text size
avoid: non-code text → body3
replacing with sans-serif style is not permitted → always use fontFamily.code
